scores.label: Budget headroom
scores.note: £5,000 under budget — no room for works

questions: Does the annexe have its own completely separate external entrance?
Is the annexe shower a walk-in/level-access shower, or does it have a step or bath?
Is there full air/smoke separation between the annexe and main house so a smoker in the annexe cannot affect the main living areas?
What is the heating fuel — oil, gas, LPG, heat pump or electric? What are approximate annual costs?
Is the drainage mains or private (septic tank / sewage treatment plant)?
What broadband speeds are available — specifically upload as well as download? Is full fibre (FTTP) available?

scores.label: Shower / wetroom
scores.note: Confirmed: walk-in mains shower in annexe (from original listing description)

scores.label: Planning / building control
scores.note: Indemnity policy to be obtained 'after survey if needed' — vendor misunderstands process; solicitor must make this a pre-exchange condition

scores.label: Fuel type
scores.note: Confirmed: oil boiler (Worcester) to main house only; annexe has independent LPG CH — ideal separation

scores.label: Drainage type
scores.note: Confirmed: traditional septic tank to soakaway (not watercourse) — 2020 GBR do not apply; emptied 2 weeks ago; compliant

scores.label: Road noise
scores.note: Agent confirms: 22-inch stone walls + garden + hedgerow; not noticeable inside with windows closed — assess at viewing

questions: Does the annexe have its own completely separate external entrance — i.e. can the occupant reach the annexe without passing through the main house at all?
Are all the annexe rooms (living/kitchen, bedroom, shower room) on a single ground level with no internal stairs?
What broadband provider does the vendor use and approximately what speeds do they get — particularly upload?
What are the approximate annual oil heating costs for the main house? When was the Worcester boiler last serviced and is there a service record?
What are the approximate annual LPG costs for the annexe? How is the LPG supplied — bottles or a fixed tank — and where is it located on the plot?
Is there an onward chain on the vendor's side?
Can the building control indemnity policy be obtained as a condition prior to exchange, rather than after survey? Our solicitor will need this confirmed early.
What is the approximate size of the paddock in acres? Is it currently grazed or maintained, and is there any form of pedestrian access to it?
red_flags: BC indemnity: vendor says 'after survey if needed' — this must be a pre-exchange condition; instruct solicitor to require it unconditionally
LPG annexe heating: confirm tank/bottle location and annual cost; budget for possible ASHP upgrade longer term
Broadband: vendor works from home 'without issues' but speeds unconfirmed — check Ofcom checker at TA20 3HG before viewing

scores.label: Physical separation
scores.note: Attached annexe — smoke separation needs confirming

scores.label: Listed building
scores.note: Grade II listed — alterations need Listed Building Consent

questions: Does the annexe have its own completely separate external entrance?
Does the annexe have a walk-in shower or wet room? If not, is there space to add one?
Is there full air/smoke separation between the annexe and main house so a smoker in the annexe cannot affect the main living areas?
Is there a walk-in or level-access shower — no step — accessible from the ground floor?
What is the heating fuel — oil, gas, LPG, heat pump or electric? What are approximate annual costs?
Is the drainage mains or private (septic tank / sewage treatment plant)?
What broadband speeds are available — specifically upload as well as download? Is full fibre (FTTP) available?
Are there any outstanding listed building consent issues or works carried out without consent? Has an LBC application been made for any works?
Is there an onward chain on the vendor's side? Are the vendors already under offer elsewhere?
red_flags: Grade II listed — ASHP, solar panels and annexe works all need Listed Building Consent (LBC)

questions: The external steps down to the property are a serious concern for a family member with limited mobility — is there any level access route to the front door, or would a ramp be feasible?

What are the approximate dimensions of the double garage?
Is there any ground floor shower or wetroom anywhere in the main house?
red_flags: EXTERNAL STEPS DOWN to property — serious mobility barrier; potentially disqualifying for elderly relative

scores.label: Flood risk

questions: The brochure confirms the annexe has its own front door but is also linked from the main house kitchen — is it possible to seal the internal connecting door permanently to create full smoke/air separation?
The annexe wet room has an electric shower (Mira Advance) — is the annexe heated solely by oil radiators from the same boiler as the main house, or does it have any independent heating control?
Has the annexe been granted planning permission and building control completion certificate? The council tax banding (Band A) suggests approved use — can the agent confirm?
What are the approximate annual oil heating costs for the whole property?
Has the property or the access road ever been affected by flooding? The property is in Westonzoyland on the Somerset Levels — what is the EA flood risk designation for TA7 0EB?
Is it insurable for flood at standard market rates, or is a Flood Re scheme needed?
What broadband speeds are available at TA7 0EB — upload as well as download? Is full fibre (FTTP) available?
Is there an onward chain on the vendor's side?
red_flags: FLOOD RISK: Westonzoyland, Somerset Levels — check gov.uk/check-long-term-flood-risk at TA7 0EB before viewing
Oil heating — single boiler serves both buildings. Budget £10k–18k net for ASHP replacement (£7,500 BUS grant available)
Internal connection between annex and main house — smoke separation needs confirming before any offer

scores.label: Physical separation
scores.note: Attached annexe — smoke separation needs confirming

scores.label: Listed building
scores.note: Grade II listed — alterations need Listed Building Consent

questions: Does the annexe have its own completely separate external entrance?
Does the annexe have a walk-in shower or wet room? If not, is there space to add one?
Is there full air/smoke separation between the annexe and main house so a smoker in the annexe cannot affect the main living areas?
Is there a walk-in or level-access shower — no step — accessible from the ground floor?
What is the heating fuel — oil, gas, LPG, heat pump or electric? What are approximate annual costs?
Is the drainage mains or private (septic tank / sewage treatment plant)?
What broadband speeds are available — specifically upload as well as download? Is full fibre (FTTP) available?
Are there any outstanding listed building consent issues or works carried out without consent? Has an LBC application been made for any works?
Is there an onward chain on the vendor's side? Are the vendors already under offer elsewhere?
red_flags: Grade II listed — ASHP, solar panels and annexe works all need Listed Building Consent (LBC)

scores.label: Ferry dependency

questions: What broadband speeds are available at the property — upload as well as download? Is Wight Fibre full fibre available here?
Does the annexe have its own completely separate external entrance with no internal connection to the main house?
Are all annexe rooms on a single ground floor level with no internal stairs?
What is the heating fuel for the annexe — gas, electric, or oil?
Is there an onward chain — have the vendors found a property to buy, or are they still searching?
FERRY CAVEAT: What is the realistic cost and time per return crossing to Southampton/Portsmouth for medical appointments? Does the property qualify for any island resident discount on ferry travel?
red_flags: FERRY DEPENDENCY — medical access, moving day and repeat visits all require ferry; significant ongoing cost and logistical complexity for elderly relative
Onward chain — vendors actively looking, no purchase agreed yet

questions: Does the lower ground floor have a separate external entrance — i.e. can it be accessed independently from outside without going through the main house?
